entries.title: NEET: Mitochondria
entries.hook: Stop missing this question in mock tests:
explanations: Double-membrane organelle with inner membrane folded into cristae. Powerhouse of the cell producing ATP via oxidative phosphorylation. Contains its own DNA.
DNA to mRNA by RNA polymerase. Pre-mRNA capped, tailed, spliced in eukaryotes. In nucleus. Initiation, elongation, termination.
Polymers of amino acids. Peptide bonds link amino acids. Primary: sequence. Secondary: alpha helix, beta sheet. Tertiary: 3D folding. Quaternary: multiple subunits.
Gas: no fixed shape/volume. Liquid: fixed volume, no shape. Solid: fixed shape and volume. Ideal gas law: PV = nRT. Real gases deviate at high pressure.

entries.title: NEET: Mendel's Laws
entries.hook: This concept is a must-know for NEET:
explanations: Segregation: alleles separate in gametes. Independent Assortment: genes on different chromosomes assort independently. Dominance: dominant allele masks recessive.
Innate: non-specific, from birth. Acquired: specific, after exposure. Active: body produces antibodies. Passive: ready-made antibodies received.
Protecting species in natural habitat. National parks, sanctuaries, biosphere reserves. Example: Jim Corbett National Park.
Derivatives of ammonia. Primary: one alkyl group. Secondary: two. Tertiary: three. Quaternary ammonium salts. Basic nature. Diazotization reaction.

entries.title: NEET: Lac Operon
entries.hook: Master this NEET concept once and for all:
explanations: Inducible operon. Lactose absent: repressor blocks transcription. Lactose present: binds repressor, allows lac gene transcription.
Gene inserted into vector. Vector introduced to host. Host expresses desired protein. Used for insulin, growth hormone, vaccines.
Electrons in quantized orbits. Angular momentum = nh/2pi. Energy levels: En = -13.6/n2 eV. Transitions produce spectral lines.
Conductivity between conductor and insulator. Doping: adding impurities. n-type: extra electrons. p-type: holes. PN junction: diode.

entries.title: NEET: Fluid Mosaic Model
entries.hook: NEET aspirants, listen up:
explanations: The plasma membrane is a phospholipid bilayer with embedded proteins. It is fluid and dynamic. Proposed by Singer and Nicolson in 1972.
Double-membrane organelle with inner membrane folded into cristae. Powerhouse of the cell producing ATP via oxidative phosphorylation. Contains its own DNA.
Work = change in kinetic energy. W = 0.5mv2 - 0.5mu2. Work = force x displacement in force direction. Unit: Joule.
Mixing of atomic orbitals to form equivalent hybrid orbitals. sp (linear), sp2 (trigonal planar), sp3 (tetrahedral). Explains bond angles.

entries.title: NEET: HIV and AIDS
entries.hook: Most students get this wrong in NEET:
explanations: HIV attacks helper T-cells. Transmitted via blood, sex, mother to child. ELISA test detects antibodies. Antiretroviral therapy controls it.
Carbon compounds. Tetravalent. Single bonds: alkane. Double: alkene. Triple:alkyne. Functional groups determine properties. IUPAC naming.
Monosaccharides: glucose, fructose. Disaccharides: sucrose, maltose. Polysaccharides: starch, cellulose. Reducing sugars have free aldehyde group.
Addition polymers: polyethylene, PVC, polystyrene. Condensation polymers: nylon, polyester, proteins. Natural: rubber, cellulose. Synthetic: plastics, fibers.

entries.title: NEET: Prokaryotic vs Eukaryotic
entries.hook: Most students get this wrong in NEET:
explanations: Prokaryotes lack a membrane-bound nucleus. Eukaryotes have a true nucleus and membrane-bound organelles. Bacteria are prokaryotes; plants, animals, fungi are eukaryotes.
Segregation: alleles separate in gametes. Independent Assortment: genes on different chromosomes assort independently. Dominance: dominant allele masks recessive.
Allele frequencies constant without evolutionary forces. p2 + 2pq + q2 = 1. Affected by mutation, gene flow, drift, selection, non-random mating.
Carbonyl group C=O. Aldehydes: terminal carbonyl. Ketones: internal. Nucleophilic addition reactions. Fehling's test distinguishes aldehydes.

entries.title: NEET: Photosynthesis Light Reaction
entries.hook: NEET topper secret: master this concept:
explanations: Occurs in thylakoids. Light splits water producing O2, ATP, NADPH. Photosystems I and II work in Z-scheme.
Hypothalamus controls pituitary. Thyroid: metabolism. Parathyroid: calcium. Adrenal: stress. Pancreas: blood sugar. Gonads: reproduction.
Oscillating electric and magnetic fields perpendicular to each other and direction of propagation. Speed = c = 3e8 m/s. Light is EM wave.
Derivatives of ammonia. Primary: one alkyl group. Secondary: two. Tertiary: three. Quaternary ammonium salts. Basic nature. Diazotization reaction.

entries.title: NEET: Calvin Cycle
entries.hook: This keeps appearing in NEET papers:
explanations: Dark reaction in stroma. CO2 fixed to glucose using ATP and NADPH. Stages: carboxylation, reduction, regeneration of RuBP.
Producers make food. Consumers eat others. Decomposers break down dead matter. Trophic levels form food chains. Energy flow is unidirectional.
Regions with high endemic species under threat. India has 4: Western Ghats, Eastern Himalayas, Indo-Burma, Sundaland.
Carbonyl group C=O. Aldehydes: terminal carbonyl. Ketones: internal. Nucleophilic addition reactions. Fehling's test distinguishes aldehydes.

entries.title: NEET: Cell Theory
entries.hook: NEET aspirants, listen up:
explanations: All living organisms are composed of cells. The cell is the basic structural and functional unit of life. All cells arise from pre-existing cells. Proposed by Schleiden, Schwann, and Virchow.
Inspiration: diaphragm contracts, intercostal muscles lift ribs. Expiration: passive relaxation. Intrapleural pressure changes drive air flow.
F = k(q1q2)/r2. Like charges repel, unlike attract. k = 9e9 Nm2/C2. Electric field E = F/q.
Forward rate = reverse rate. Kc = products/reactants. Le Chatelier's principle: system shifts to oppose change. Effect of temperature, pressure, concentration.

entries.title: NEET: Prokaryotic vs Eukaryotic
entries.hook: This concept is a must-know for NEET:
explanations: Prokaryotes lack a membrane-bound nucleus. Eukaryotes have a true nucleus and membrane-bound organelles. Bacteria are prokaryotes; plants, animals, fungi are eukaryotes.
RER has ribosomes and synthesizes proteins. SER lacks ribosomes, synthesizes lipids, detoxifies poisons, stores calcium ions.
HIV attacks helper T-cells. Transmitted via blood, sex, mother to child. ELISA test detects antibodies. Antiretroviral therapy controls it.
First: elliptical orbits with Sun at focus. Second: equal areas in equal time. Third: T2 proportional to a3.

entries.title: NEET: Crossing Over
entries.hook: Stop missing this question in mock tests:
explanations: Exchange of genetic material between homologous chromosomes in prophase I of meiosis. Increases genetic variation. Occurs at chiasmata.
mRNA to protein at ribosomes. tRNA carries amino acids. Codon-anticodon pairing. AUG start codon. Initiation, elongation, termination.
First: inertia. Second: F=ma. Third: action-reaction. Inertia resists change in motion.
Angle of incidence = angle of reflection. Mirror formula: 1/f = 1/u + 1/v. Concave: real/inverted. Convex: virtual/upright.

entries.title: NEET: Ohm's Law
entries.hook: Most students get this wrong in NEET:
explanations: V = IR. Current proportional to voltage at constant temperature. Resistance depends on material, length, cross-section, temperature.
Rutherford: nucleus at center with electrons orbiting. Bohr: quantized energy levels. Thomson: plum pudding model. Chadwick: discovered neutron.
Enthalpy H = U + PV. Exothermic: dH negative. Endothermic: dH positive. Gibbs free energy dG = dH - TdS. Spontaneous when dG negative.
Polymers of amino acids. Peptide bonds link amino acids. Primary: sequence. Secondary: alpha helix, beta sheet. Tertiary: 3D folding. Quaternary: multiple subunits.

entries.title: NEET: Human Evolution
entries.hook: This concept is a must-know for NEET:
explanations: Australopithecus 4 mya, Homo habilis 2.4 mya, Homo erectus 1.8 mya, Neanderthals 0.4 mya, Homo sapiens 0.3 mya. Originated in Africa.
Energy cannot be created or destroyed, only converted. Total mechanical energy (KE+PE) constant without non-conservative forces.
F = G(m1m2)/r2. G = 6.67e-11 Nm2/kg2. g = 9.8 m/s2 on Earth. g varies with altitude, depth, latitude.
In fluid flow, higher velocity = lower pressure. Explains airplane lift, atomizer, venturi meter. P + 0.5pv2 + pgh = constant.

entries.title: NEET: Fluid Mosaic Model
entries.hook: Stop missing this question in mock tests:
explanations: The plasma membrane is a phospholipid bilayer with embedded proteins. It is fluid and dynamic. Proposed by Singer and Nicolson in 1972.
RuBisCO fixes O2 instead of CO2 producing toxic compound. Wasteful process reducing photosynthesis. Common in C3 plants under hot dry conditions.
Allele frequencies constant without evolutionary forces. p2 + 2pq + q2 = 1. Affected by mutation, gene flow, drift, selection, non-random mating.
DC: constant direction. AC: periodically reverses. India: 230V, 50Hz. RMS voltage = peak/sqrt(2). AC can be transformed.

entries.title: NEET: Immunity Types
entries.hook: NEET aspirants, listen up:
explanations: Innate: non-specific, from birth. Acquired: specific, after exposure. Active: body produces antibodies. Passive: ready-made antibodies received.
Restriction enzymes cut DNA. Vectors: plasmids, viruses. PCR amplifies DNA. Gel electrophoresis separates. Sequencing reads genetic code.
V = IR. Current proportional to voltage at constant temperature. Resistance depends on material, length, cross-section, temperature.
Gas: no fixed shape/volume. Liquid: fixed volume, no shape. Solid: fixed shape and volume. Ideal gas law: PV = nRT. Real gases deviate at high pressure.

explanations: Functional unit of kidney. Bowman's capsule filters blood. PCT reabsorbs nutrients. Loop of Henle concentrates urine. DCT adjusts electrolyte balance.
Mechanical waves need medium. Transverse: particles perpendicular to wave. Longitudinal: particles parallel. v = f*lambda.
Alkanes: CnH2n+2, saturated. Alkenes: CnH2n, unsaturated. Alkynes: CnH2n-2. Aromatic: benzene ring. Combustion, substitution, addition reactions.
Transition metals (d-block): variable oxidation states, colored compounds, catalytic properties. Lanthanoids and actinoids (f-block): inner transition elements.

entries.title: NEET: Cardiac Cycle
entries.hook: Most students get this wrong in NEET:
explanations: Atrial systole 0.1s, ventricular systole 0.3s, joint diastole 0.4s. Total 0.8s at 72 bpm. Heart sounds from valve closure.
Energy cannot be created or destroyed, only converted. Total mechanical energy (KE+PE) constant without non-conservative forces.
F = G(m1m2)/r2. G = 6.67e-11 Nm2/kg2. g = 9.8 m/s2 on Earth. g varies with altitude, depth, latitude.
Carbonyl group C=O. Aldehydes: terminal carbonyl. Ketones: internal. Nucleophilic addition reactions. Fehling's test distinguishes aldehydes.

entries.script: Cardiac Cycle. Atrial systole 0.1s, ventricular systole 0.3s, joint diastole 0.4s. Total 0.8s at 72 bpm. Heart sounds from valve closure. Conservation of Energy. Energy cannot be created or destroyed, only converted. Total mechanical energy (KE+PE) constant without non-conservative forces. Gravitation. F = G(m1m2)/r2. G = 6.67e-11 Nm2/kg2. g = 9.8 m/s2 on Earth. g varies with altitude, depth, latitude. Aldehydes and Ketones. Carbonyl group C=O. Aldehydes: terminal carbonyl. Ketones: internal. Nucleophilic addition reactions. Fehling's test distinguishes aldehydes.

entries.title: NEET: Transcription
entries.hook: Stop missing this question in mock tests:
explanations: DNA to mRNA by RNA polymerase. Pre-mRNA capped, tailed, spliced in eukaryotes. In nucleus. Initiation, elongation, termination.
Uncontrolled cell division from genetic mutations. Proto-oncogenes promote growth. Tumor suppressors inhibit. Treatments: surgery, radiation, chemo, immunotherapy.
Regions with high endemic species under threat. India has 4: Western Ghats, Eastern Himalayas, Indo-Burma, Sundaland.
Oxidation: loss of electrons. Reduction: gain of electrons. Oxidizing agent accepts electrons. Reducing agent donates electrons. Balancing half-reactions.

explanations: RuBisCO fixes O2 instead of CO2 producing toxic compound. Wasteful process reducing photosynthesis. Common in C3 plants under hot dry conditions.
Atrial systole 0.1s, ventricular systole 0.3s, joint diastole 0.4s. Total 0.8s at 72 bpm. Heart sounds from valve closure.
Allele frequencies constant without evolutionary forces. p2 + 2pq + q2 = 1. Affected by mutation, gene flow, drift, selection, non-random mating.

entries.title: NEET: DNA Structure
entries.hook: Master this NEET concept once and for all:
explanations: Double helix. Nucleotides: phosphate, deoxyribose, base (A,T,G,C). A=T (2 bonds), G≡C (3 bonds). Antiparallel strands.
Carbon: photosynthesis + respiration. Nitrogen: fixation, nitrification, assimilation, ammonification, denitrification. Water: evaporation, condensation, precipitation.
Conductivity between conductor and insulator. Doping: adding impurities. n-type: extra electrons. p-type: holes. PN junction: diode.
Central metal ion bonded to ligands. Coordination number. Chelate effect. Crystal field theory explains color and magnetism. Important in bioinorganic chemistry.

entries.title: NEET: Oxygen Transport
entries.hook: Stop missing this question in mock tests:
explanations: 98.5% O2 bound to hemoglobin as oxyhemoglobin. 1.5% dissolved in plasma. Each hemoglobin carries 4 O2. Dissociation curve is sigmoid.
Mechanical waves need medium. Transverse: particles perpendicular to wave. Longitudinal: particles parallel. v = f*lambda.
Snell's law: n1sin(i) = n2sin(r). Light bends toward normal in denser medium. Refractive index n = c/v. Total internal reflection above critical angle.
Electrons in quantized orbits. Angular momentum = nh/2pi. Energy levels: En = -13.6/n2 eV. Transitions produce spectral lines.

entries.title: NEET: Fluid Mosaic Model
entries.hook: Most students get this wrong in NEET:
explanations: The plasma membrane is a phospholipid bilayer with embedded proteins. It is fluid and dynamic. Proposed by Singer and Nicolson in 1972.
Dendrites receive signals. Cell body integrates. Axon transmits. Myelin sheath speeds conduction. Synapse is junction between neurons.
Innate: non-specific, from birth. Acquired: specific, after exposure. Active: body produces antibodies. Passive: ready-made antibodies received.
Alcohols: -OH group. Phenols: -OH on benzene. Ethers: R-O-R. Preparation from alkenes, haloalkanes. Oxidation: alcohol to aldehyde to carboxylic acid.

entries.title: NEET: Mendel's Laws
entries.hook: Master this NEET concept once and for all:
explanations: Segregation: alleles separate in gametes. Independent Assortment: genes on different chromosomes assort independently. Dominance: dominant allele masks recessive.
Restriction enzymes cut DNA. Vectors: plasmids, viruses. PCR amplifies DNA. Gel electrophoresis separates. Sequencing reads genetic code.
Mixing of atomic orbitals to form equivalent hybrid orbitals. sp (linear), sp2 (trigonal planar), sp3 (tetrahedral). Explains bond angles.
Alcohols: -OH group. Phenols: -OH on benzene. Ethers: R-O-R. Preparation from alkenes, haloalkanes. Oxidation: alcohol to aldehyde to carboxylic acid.

entries.title: NEET: Breathing Mechanism
entries.hook: NEET aspirants, listen up:
explanations: Inspiration: diaphragm contracts, intercostal muscles lift ribs. Expiration: passive relaxation. Intrapleural pressure changes drive air flow.
Functional unit of kidney. Bowman's capsule filters blood. PCT reabsorbs nutrients. Loop of Henle concentrates urine. DCT adjusts electrolyte balance.
mRNA to protein at ribosomes. tRNA carries amino acids. Codon-anticodon pairing. AUG start codon. Initiation, elongation, termination.
Valence Shell Electron Pair Repulsion. Electron pairs arrange to minimize repulsion. Determines molecular geometry. Examples: linear, bent, tetrahedral.

entries.title: NEET: Calvin Cycle
entries.hook: This concept is a must-know for NEET:
explanations: Dark reaction in stroma. CO2 fixed to glucose using ATP and NADPH. Stages: carboxylation, reduction, regeneration of RuBP.
First: inertia. Second: F=ma. Third: action-reaction. Inertia resists change in motion.
Electrochemical cells convert chemical to electrical energy. Anode: oxidation. Cathode: reduction. EMF = Ered(cathode) - Ered(anode). Nernst equation.
Monosaccharides: glucose, fructose. Disaccharides: sucrose, maltose. Polysaccharides: starch, cellulose. Reducing sugars have free aldehyde group.

entries.title: NEET: Prokaryotic vs Eukaryotic
entries.hook: Master this NEET concept once and for all:
explanations: Prokaryotes lack a membrane-bound nucleus. Eukaryotes have a true nucleus and membrane-bound organelles. Bacteria are prokaryotes; plants, animals, fungi are eukaryotes.
Gene inserted into vector. Vector introduced to host. Host expresses desired protein. Used for insulin, growth hormone, vaccines.
F = k(q1q2)/r2. Like charges repel, unlike attract. k = 9e9 Nm2/C2. Electric field E = F/q.
Forward rate = reverse rate. Kc = products/reactants. Le Chatelier's principle: system shifts to oppose change. Effect of temperature, pressure, concentration.

entries.title: NEET: Endoplasmic Reticulum
entries.hook: This keeps appearing in NEET papers:
explanations: RER has ribosomes and synthesizes proteins. SER lacks ribosomes, synthesizes lipids, detoxifies poisons, stores calcium ions.
Crassulacean acid metabolism. Stomata open at night to fix CO2 into malate. Day: CO2 released for Calvin cycle. Adaptation for arid conditions.
Ionic: electron transfer, electrostatic attraction. Covalent: electron sharing. Metallic: delocalized electrons. VSEPR theory predicts shape.
-COOH group. Weak acids. Reactions: esterification, reduction, decarboxylation. Acetic acid most common. Formic acid in ant venom.

Reactions: esterification, reduction, decarboxylation. Acetic acid most common. Formic acid in ant venom.

entries.title: NEET: Cell Cycle
entries.hook: High-yield topic for your NEET exam:
explanations: Interphase (G1, S, G2) prepares for division. Mitosis divides nucleus. Cytokinesis divides cytoplasm. G0 is resting phase.
Periodic motion where restoring force proportional to displacement. T = 2pi*sqrt(m/k). Examples: spring, pendulum.
Electrons in quantized orbits. Angular momentum = nh/2pi. Energy levels: En = -13.6/n2 eV. Transitions produce spectral lines.
Alkanes: CnH2n+2, saturated. Alkenes: CnH2n, unsaturated. Alkynes: CnH2n-2. Aromatic: benzene ring. Combustion, substitution, addition reactions.

entries.title: NEET: Work-Energy Theorem
entries.hook: NEET aspirants, listen up:
explanations: Work = change in kinetic energy. W = 0.5mv2 - 0.5mu2. Work = force x displacement in force direction. Unit: Joule.
F = k(q1q2)/r2. Like charges repel, unlike attract. k = 9e9 Nm2/C2. Electric field E = F/q.
Group 1 (alkali metals) and Group 2 (alkaline earth metals). Highly reactive. ns1 and ns2 configuration. Hydration enthalpy decreases down group.
Groups 13-18. Boron family to noble gases. Variable oxidation states. Trends in metallic character, ionization energy, electronegativity down groups.

entries.title: NEET: Calvin Cycle
entries.hook: Stop missing this question in mock tests:
explanations: Dark reaction in stroma. CO2 fixed to glucose using ATP and NADPH. Stages: carboxylation, reduction, regeneration of RuBP.
P wave: atrial depolarization. QRS: ventricular depolarization. T wave: ventricular repolarization. Records electrical activity of heart.
Alkanes: CnH2n+2, saturated. Alkenes: CnH2n, unsaturated. Alkynes: CnH2n-2. Aromatic: benzene ring. Combustion, substitution, addition reactions.
Transition metals (d-block): variable oxidation states, colored compounds, catalytic properties. Lanthanoids and actinoids (f-block): inner transition elements.

entries.title: NEET: Thermodynamics First Law
entries.hook: This keeps appearing in NEET papers:
explanations: Energy cannot be created or destroyed. dU = dQ - dW. Internal energy change = heat added - work done.
Most efficient heat engine. Efficiency = 1 - T2/T1. Reversible cyclic process. Carnot cycle: isothermal + adiabatic expansions and compressions.
Carbon compounds. Tetravalent. Single bonds: alkane. Double: alkene. Triple:alkyne. Functional groups determine properties. IUPAC naming.
Alcohols: -OH group. Phenols: -OH on benzene. Ethers: R-O-R. Preparation from alkenes, haloalkanes. Oxidation: alcohol to aldehyde to carboxylic acid.

entries.hook: This concept is a must-know for NEET:
explanations: The plasma membrane is a phospholipid bilayer with embedded proteins. It is fluid and dynamic. Proposed by Singer and Nicolson in 1972.
Innate: non-specific, from birth. Acquired: specific, after exposure. Active: body produces antibodies. Passive: ready-made antibodies received.
First: inertia. Second: F=ma. Third: action-reaction. Inertia resists change in motion.
F = qvBsin(theta). Moving charge in magnetic field experiences force. Lorentz force: F = q(E + vxB).

entries.title: NEET: Prokaryotic vs Eukaryotic
entries.hook: High-yield topic for your NEET exam:
explanations: Prokaryotes lack a membrane-bound nucleus. Eukaryotes have a true nucleus and membrane-bound organelles. Bacteria are prokaryotes; plants, animals, fungi are eukaryotes.
mRNA to protein at ribosomes. tRNA carries amino acids. Codon-anticodon pairing. AUG start codon. Initiation, elongation, termination.
Energy cannot be created or destroyed. dU = dQ - dW. Internal energy change = heat added - work done.
Oxidation: loss of electrons. Reduction: gain of electrons. Oxidizing agent accepts electrons. Reducing agent donates electrons. Balancing half-reactions.

Bacteria are prokaryotes; plants, animals, fungi are eukaryotes. Translation. mRNA to protein at ribosomes. tRNA carries amino acids. Codon-anticodon pairing. AUG start codon. Initiation, elongation, termination. Thermodynamics First Law. Energy cannot be created or destroyed. dU = dQ - dW. Internal energy change = heat added - work done. Redox Reactions. Oxidation: loss of electrons. Reduction: gain of electrons. Oxidizing agent accepts electrons. Reducing agent donates electrons. Balancing half-reactions.

entries.title: NEET: Immunity Types
entries.hook: Master this NEET concept once and for all:
explanations: Innate: non-specific, from birth. Acquired: specific, after exposure. Active: body produces antibodies. Passive: ready-made antibodies received.
Carbon: photosynthesis + respiration. Nitrogen: fixation, nitrification, assimilation, ammonification, denitrification. Water: evaporation, condensation, precipitation.
Electrochemical cells convert chemical to electrical energy. Anode: oxidation. Cathode: reduction. EMF = Ered(cathode) - Ered(anode). Nernst equation.
Transition metals (d-block): variable oxidation states, colored compounds, catalytic properties. Lanthanoids and actinoids (f-block): inner transition elements.

Ionic: electron transfer, electrostatic attraction. Covalent: electron sharing. Metallic: delocalized electrons. VSEPR theory predicts shape.
-COOH group. Weak acids. Reactions: esterification, reduction, decarboxylation. Acetic acid most common. Formic acid in ant venom.

entries.title: NEET: Crossing Over
entries.hook: This keeps appearing in NEET papers:
explanations: Exchange of genetic material between homologous chromosomes in prophase I of meiosis. Increases genetic variation. Occurs at chiasmata.
Crassulacean acid metabolism. Stomata open at night to fix CO2 into malate. Day: CO2 released for Calvin cycle. Adaptation for arid conditions.
DC: constant direction. AC: periodically reverses. India: 230V, 50Hz. RMS voltage = peak/sqrt(2). AC can be transformed.
Electrons in quantized orbits. Angular momentum = nh/2pi. Energy levels: En = -13.6/n2 eV. Transitions produce spectral lines.

entries.title: NEET: HIV and AIDS
entries.hook: NEET aspirants, listen up:
explanations: HIV attacks helper T-cells. Transmitted via blood, sex, mother to child. ELISA test detects antibodies. Antiretroviral therapy controls it.
Uncontrolled cell division from genetic mutations. Proto-oncogenes promote growth. Tumor suppressors inhibit. Treatments: surgery, radiation, chemo, immunotherapy.
V = IR. Current proportional to voltage at constant temperature. Resistance depends on material, length, cross-section, temperature.
Central metal ion bonded to ligands. Coordination number. Chelate effect. Crystal field theory explains color and magnetism. Important in bioinorganic chemistry.

entries.title: NEET: Endoplasmic Reticulum
entries.hook: Master this NEET concept once and for all:
explanations: RER has ribosomes and synthesizes proteins. SER lacks ribosomes, synthesizes lipids, detoxifies poisons, stores calcium ions.
Most efficient heat engine. Efficiency = 1 - T2/T1. Reversible cyclic process. Carnot cycle: isothermal + adiabatic expansions and compressions.
Rutherford: nucleus at center with electrons orbiting. Bohr: quantized energy levels. Thomson: plum pudding model. Chadwick: discovered neutron.
Alkanes: CnH2n+2, saturated. Alkenes: CnH2n, unsaturated. Alkynes: CnH2n-2. Aromatic: benzene ring. Combustion, substitution, addition reactions.

F = G(m1m2)/r2. G = 6.67e-11 Nm2/kg2. g = 9.8 m/s2 on Earth. g varies with altitude, depth, latitude.
Mechanical waves need medium. Transverse: particles perpendicular to wave. Longitudinal: particles parallel. v = f*lambda.

entries.title: NEET: Bernoulli's Principle
entries.hook: High-yield topic for your NEET exam:
explanations: In fluid flow, higher velocity = lower pressure. Explains airplane lift, atomizer, venturi meter. P + 0.5pv2 + pgh = constant.
Energy cannot be created or destroyed. dU = dQ - dW. Internal energy change = heat added - work done.
Oxidation: loss of electrons. Reduction: gain of electrons. Oxidizing agent accepts electrons. Reducing agent donates electrons. Balancing half-reactions.
Rate of reaction = d[conc]/dt. Order: sum of exponents in rate law. Activation energy: minimum energy for reaction. Catalyst lowers activation energy.

entries.title: NEET: Endoplasmic Reticulum
entries.hook: High-yield topic for your NEET exam:
explanations: RER has ribosomes and synthesizes proteins. SER lacks ribosomes, synthesizes lipids, detoxifies poisons, stores calcium ions.
RuBisCO fixes O2 instead of CO2 producing toxic compound. Wasteful process reducing photosynthesis. Common in C3 plants under hot dry conditions.
98.5% O2 bound to hemoglobin as oxyhemoglobin. 1.5% dissolved in plasma. Each hemoglobin carries 4 O2. Dissociation curve is sigmoid.
Gene inserted into vector. Vector introduced to host. Host expresses desired protein. Used for insulin, growth hormone, vaccines.

Disaccharides: sucrose, maltose. Polysaccharides: starch, cellulose. Reducing sugars have free aldehyde group. Coordination Compounds. Central metal ion bonded to ligands. Coordination number. Chelate effect. Crystal field theory explains color and magnetism. Important in bioinorganic chemistry.

F = G(m1m2)/r2. G = 6.67e-11 Nm2/kg2. g = 9.8 m/s2 on Earth. g varies with altitude, depth, latitude.

entries.title: NEET: Mendel's Laws
entries.hook: Most students get this wrong in NEET:
explanations: Segregation: alleles separate in gametes. Independent Assortment: genes on different chromosomes assort independently. Dominance: dominant allele masks recessive.
First: inertia. Second: F=ma. Third: action-reaction. Inertia resists change in motion.
Periodic motion where restoring force proportional to displacement. T = 2pi*sqrt(m/k). Examples: spring, pendulum.
Transition metals (d-block): variable oxidation states, colored compounds, catalytic properties. Lanthanoids and actinoids (f-block): inner transition elements.

entries.title: NEET: Photosynthesis Light Reaction
entries.hook: This keeps appearing in NEET papers:
explanations: Occurs in thylakoids. Light splits water producing O2, ATP, NADPH. Photosystems I and II work in Z-scheme.
Innate: non-specific, from birth. Acquired: specific, after exposure. Active: body produces antibodies. Passive: ready-made antibodies received.
Energy cannot be created or destroyed, only converted. Total mechanical energy (KE+PE) constant without non-conservative forces.
Carbonyl group C=O. Aldehydes: terminal carbonyl. Ketones: internal. Nucleophilic addition reactions. Fehling's test distinguishes aldehydes.

entries.title: NEET: Second Law of Thermodynamics
entries.hook: NEET aspirants, listen up:
explanations: Entropy of universe always increases. Heat cannot spontaneously flow from cold to hot. Perpetual motion machines impossible.
V = IR. Current proportional to voltage at constant temperature. Resistance depends on material, length, cross-section, temperature.
Snell's law: n1sin(i) = n2sin(r). Light bends toward normal in denser medium. Refractive index n = c/v. Total internal reflection above critical angle.
Ionic: electron transfer, electrostatic attraction. Covalent: electron sharing. Metallic: delocalized electrons. VSEPR theory predicts shape.

entries.title: NEET: Cell Cycle
entries.hook: Stop missing this question in mock tests:
explanations: Interphase (G1, S, G2) prepares for division. Mitosis divides nucleus. Cytokinesis divides cytoplasm. G0 is resting phase.
Numbers: count at each level. Biomass: mass at each level. Energy: energy at each level. Energy pyramid always upright.
Mechanical waves need medium. Transverse: particles perpendicular to wave. Longitudinal: particles parallel. v = f*lambda.
Forward rate = reverse rate. Kc = products/reactants. Le Chatelier's principle: system shifts to oppose change. Effect of temperature, pressure, concentration.

entries.title: NEET: Translation
entries.hook: Master this NEET concept once and for all:
explanations: mRNA to protein at ribosomes. tRNA carries amino acids. Codon-anticodon pairing. AUG start codon. Initiation, elongation, termination.
Restriction enzymes cut DNA. Vectors: plasmids, viruses. PCR amplifies DNA. Gel electrophoresis separates. Sequencing reads genetic code.
Angle of incidence = angle of reflection. Mirror formula: 1/f = 1/u + 1/v. Concave: real/inverted. Convex: virtual/upright.
Light ejects electrons from metal surface. Einstein: light as photons, E = hf. Kinetic energy of ejected electron = hf - work function.

entries.title: NEET: Carnot Engine

explanations: Most efficient heat engine. Efficiency = 1 - T2/T1. Reversible cyclic process. Carnot cycle: isothermal + adiabatic expansions and compressions.
Changing magnetic flux induces EMF. EMF = -d(flux)/dt. Lenz's law: induced current opposes change. Basis of generators.
Rutherford: nucleus at center with electrons orbiting. Bohr: quantized energy levels. Thomson: plum pudding model. Chadwick: discovered neutron.
Monosaccharides: glucose, fructose. Disaccharides: sucrose, maltose. Polysaccharides: starch, cellulose. Reducing sugars have free aldehyde group.

Mixing of atomic orbitals to form equivalent hybrid orbitals. sp (linear), sp2 (trigonal planar), sp3 (tetrahedral). Explains bond angles.
Forward rate = reverse rate. Kc = products/reactants. Le Chatelier's principle: system shifts to oppose change. Effect of temperature, pressure, concentration.
Groups 13-18. Boron family to noble gases. Variable oxidation states. Trends in metallic character, ionization energy, electronegativity down groups.

entries.title: NEET: Crossing Over
entries.hook: Master this NEET concept once and for all:
explanations: Exchange of genetic material between homologous chromosomes in prophase I of meiosis. Increases genetic variation. Occurs at chiasmata.

Changing magnetic flux induces EMF. EMF = -d(flux)/dt. Lenz's law: induced current opposes change. Basis of generators.

entries.title: NEET: Cell Theory
entries.hook: This keeps appearing in NEET papers:
explanations: All living organisms are composed of cells. The cell is the basic structural and functional unit of life. All cells arise from pre-existing cells. Proposed by Schleiden, Schwann, and Virchow.
Completed 2003. Mapped 3 billion base pairs. 20,000-25,000 protein-coding genes. Only 1.5% DNA codes for proteins.
Carbon compounds. Tetravalent. Single bonds: alkane. Double: alkene. Triple:alkyne. Functional groups determine properties. IUPAC naming.
Derivatives of ammonia. Primary: one alkyl group. Secondary: two. Tertiary: three. Quaternary ammonium salts. Basic nature. Diazotization reaction.

entries.title: NEET: Endoplasmic Reticulum
entries.hook: This concept is a must-know for NEET:
explanations: RER has ribosomes and synthesizes proteins. SER lacks ribosomes, synthesizes lipids, detoxifies poisons, stores calcium ions.
Suicide bags containing hydrolytic enzymes. Digest worn-out organelles and foreign particles. Enzymes work best at acidic pH.
P wave: atrial depolarization. QRS: ventricular depolarization. T wave: ventricular repolarization. Records electrical activity of heart.
Central metal ion bonded to ligands. Coordination number. Chelate effect. Crystal field theory explains color and magnetism. Important in bioinorganic chemistry.

entries.title: NEET: CO2 Transport
entries.hook: NEET topper secret: master this concept:
explanations: 70% as bicarbonate ions. 20-25% as carbaminohemoglobin. 7% dissolved in plasma. Transported from tissues to lungs.
Gene inserted into vector. Vector introduced to host. Host expresses desired protein. Used for insulin, growth hormone, vaccines.
Most efficient heat engine. Efficiency = 1 - T2/T1. Reversible cyclic process. Carnot cycle: isothermal + adiabatic expansions and compressions.
Gas: no fixed shape/volume. Liquid: fixed volume, no shape. Solid: fixed shape and volume. Ideal gas law: PV = nRT. Real gases deviate at high pressure.

Numbers: count at each level. Biomass: mass at each level. Energy: energy at each level. Energy pyramid always upright.

entries.title: NEET: Hardy-Weinberg Principle
entries.hook: NEET aspirants, listen up:
explanations: Allele frequencies constant without evolutionary forces. p2 + 2pq + q2 = 1. Affected by mutation, gene flow, drift, selection, non-random mating.
Junction rule: sum of currents entering = sum leaving. Loop rule: sum of potential differences in closed loop = 0.
Polymers of amino acids. Peptide bonds link amino acids. Primary: sequence. Secondary: alpha helix, beta sheet. Tertiary: 3D folding. Quaternary: multiple subunits.
Gas: no fixed shape/volume. Liquid: fixed volume, no shape. Solid: fixed shape and volume. Ideal gas law: PV = nRT. Real gases deviate at high pressure.

entries.title: NEET: Neuron
entries.hook: Most students get this wrong in NEET:
explanations: Dendrites receive signals. Cell body integrates. Axon transmits. Myelin sheath speeds conduction. Synapse is junction between neurons.
Allele frequencies constant without evolutionary forces. p2 + 2pq + q2 = 1. Affected by mutation, gene flow, drift, selection, non-random mating.
Uncontrolled cell division from genetic mutations. Proto-oncogenes promote growth. Tumor suppressors inhibit. Treatments: surgery, radiation, chemo, immunotherapy.
Work = change in kinetic energy. W = 0.5mv2 - 0.5mu2. Work = force x displacement in force direction. Unit: Joule.

Mitosis: 2 identical diploid cells for growth. Meiosis: 4 non-identical haploid gametes with two divisions. Meiosis creates genetic variation.
Glomerular filtration 125 mL/min. Tubular reabsorption 99%. Tubular secretion. ADH increases water reabsorption. Aldosterone increases Na+ reabsorption.
Favorable traits survive and reproduce more leading to evolution. Evidences: fossils, homologous organs, embryology, molecular biology.

Decomposers break down dead matter. Trophic levels form food chains. Energy flow is unidirectional.

entries.hook: High-yield topic for your NEET exam:
explanations: Carbon: photosynthesis + respiration. Nitrogen: fixation, nitrification, assimilation, ammonification, denitrification. Water: evaporation, condensation, precipitation.
Junction rule: sum of currents entering = sum leaving. Loop rule: sum of potential differences in closed loop = 0.
Alkanes: CnH2n+2, saturated. Alkenes: CnH2n, unsaturated. Alkynes: CnH2n-2. Aromatic: benzene ring. Combustion, substitution, addition reactions.
Monosaccharides: glucose, fructose. Disaccharides: sucrose, maltose. Polysaccharides: starch, cellulose. Reducing sugars have free aldehyde group.

explanations: RER has ribosomes and synthesizes proteins. SER lacks ribosomes, synthesizes lipids, detoxifies poisons, stores calcium ions.
Segregation: alleles separate in gametes. Independent Assortment: genes on different chromosomes assort independently. Dominance: dominant allele masks recessive.
First: inertia. Second: F=ma. Third: action-reaction. Inertia resists change in motion.
Changing magnetic flux induces EMF. EMF = -d(flux)/dt. Lenz's law: induced current opposes change. Basis of generators.

entries.title: NEET: Cell Cycle
entries.hook: This concept is a must-know for NEET:
explanations: Interphase (G1, S, G2) prepares for division. Mitosis divides nucleus. Cytokinesis divides cytoplasm. G0 is resting phase.
RuBisCO fixes O2 instead of CO2 producing toxic compound. Wasteful process reducing photosynthesis. Common in C3 plants under hot dry conditions.
Work = change in kinetic energy. W = 0.5mv2 - 0.5mu2. Work = force x displacement in force direction. Unit: Joule.
Rutherford: nucleus at center with electrons orbiting. Bohr: quantized energy levels. Thomson: plum pudding model. Chadwick: discovered neutron.

Bohr: quantized energy levels. Thomson: plum pudding model. Chadwick: discovered neutron. Thermodynamics in Chemistry. Enthalpy H = U + PV. Exothermic: dH negative. Endothermic: dH positive. Gibbs free energy dG = dH - TdS. Spontaneous when dG negative.

entries.title: NEET: Mitosis vs Meiosis
entries.hook: This keeps appearing in NEET papers:
explanations: Mitosis: 2 identical diploid cells for growth. Meiosis: 4 non-identical haploid gametes with two divisions. Meiosis creates genetic variation.
Crassulacean acid metabolism. Stomata open at night to fix CO2 into malate. Day: CO2 released for Calvin cycle. Adaptation for arid conditions.
Action potential triggers Ca2+ influx. Neurotransmitters released, bind to receptors on postsynaptic membrane causing excitation or inhibition.
Groups 13-18. Boron family to noble gases. Variable oxidation states. Trends in metallic character, ionization energy, electronegativity down groups.

entries.hook: NEET aspirants, listen up:
explanations: Atrial systole 0.1s, ventricular systole 0.3s, joint diastole 0.4s. Total 0.8s at 72 bpm. Heart sounds from valve closure.
F = k(q1q2)/r2. Like charges repel, unlike attract. k = 9e9 Nm2/C2. Electric field E = F/q.
Oscillating electric and magnetic fields perpendicular to each other and direction of propagation. Speed = c = 3e8 m/s. Light is EM wave.

entries.title: NEET: Cell Cycle
entries.hook: Master this NEET concept once and for all:
explanations: Interphase (G1, S, G2) prepares for division. Mitosis divides nucleus. Cytokinesis divides cytoplasm. G0 is resting phase.
Numbers: count at each level. Biomass: mass at each level. Energy: energy at each level. Energy pyramid always upright.
Carbon: photosynthesis + respiration. Nitrogen: fixation, nitrification, assimilation, ammonification, denitrification. Water: evaporation, condensation, precipitation.
Junction rule: sum of currents entering = sum leaving. Loop rule: sum of potential differences in closed loop = 0.

entries.title: NEET: Endoplasmic Reticulum
entries.hook: Stop missing this question in mock tests:
explanations: RER has ribosomes and synthesizes proteins. SER lacks ribosomes, synthesizes lipids, detoxifies poisons, stores calcium ions.
Water rises in xylem due to transpiration from leaves. Cohesion and adhesion create continuous water column. Main force for water ascent.
Entropy of universe always increases. Heat cannot spontaneously flow from cold to hot. Perpetual motion machines impossible.
Changing magnetic flux induces EMF. EMF = -d(flux)/dt. Lenz's law: induced current opposes change. Basis of generators.

entries.title: NEET: Hardy-Weinberg Principle
entries.hook: Stop missing this question in mock tests:
explanations: Allele frequencies constant without evolutionary forces. p2 + 2pq + q2 = 1. Affected by mutation, gene flow, drift, selection, non-random mating.
Restriction enzymes cut DNA. Vectors: plasmids, viruses. PCR amplifies DNA. Gel electrophoresis separates. Sequencing reads genetic code.
Protecting species in natural habitat. National parks, sanctuaries, biosphere reserves. Example: Jim Corbett National Park.
Transition metals (d-block): variable oxidation states, colored compounds, catalytic properties. Lanthanoids and actinoids (f-block): inner transition elements.

entries.title: NEET: Lysosomes
entries.hook: Most students get this wrong in NEET:
explanations: Suicide bags containing hydrolytic enzymes. Digest worn-out organelles and foreign particles. Enzymes work best at acidic pH.
mRNA to protein at ribosomes. tRNA carries amino acids. Codon-anticodon pairing. AUG start codon. Initiation, elongation, termination.
Light ejects electrons from metal surface. Einstein: light as photons, E = hf. Kinetic energy of ejected electron = hf - work function.
Oxidation: loss of electrons. Reduction: gain of electrons. Oxidizing agent accepts electrons. Reducing agent donates electrons. Balancing half-reactions.

entries.title: NEET: Immunity Types
entries.hook: This keeps appearing in NEET papers:
explanations: Innate: non-specific, from birth. Acquired: specific, after exposure. Active: body produces antibodies. Passive: ready-made antibodies received.
Carbonyl group C=O. Aldehydes: terminal carbonyl. Ketones: internal. Nucleophilic addition reactions. Fehling's test distinguishes aldehydes.
Derivatives of ammonia. Primary: one alkyl group. Secondary: two. Tertiary: three. Quaternary ammonium salts. Basic nature. Diazotization reaction.
Monosaccharides: glucose, fructose. Disaccharides: sucrose, maltose. Polysaccharides: starch, cellulose. Reducing sugars have free aldehyde group.

explanations: Regions with high endemic species under threat. India has 4: Western Ghats, Eastern Himalayas, Indo-Burma, Sundaland.
1/f = 1/v - 1/u. Power P = 1/f (diopters). Convex: converging, positive power. Concave: diverging, negative power. Lens maker's formula.
Valence Shell Electron Pair Repulsion. Electron pairs arrange to minimize repulsion. Determines molecular geometry. Examples: linear, bent, tetrahedral.
Electrochemical cells convert chemical to electrical energy. Anode: oxidation. Cathode: reduction. EMF = Ered(cathode) - Ered(anode). Nernst equation.

entries.title: NEET: Cell Theory
entries.hook: This keeps appearing in NEET papers:
explanations: All living organisms are composed of cells. The cell is the basic structural and functional unit of life. All cells arise from pre-existing cells. Proposed by Schleiden, Schwann, and Virchow.
Enthalpy H = U + PV. Exothermic: dH negative. Endothermic: dH positive. Gibbs free energy dG = dH - TdS. Spontaneous when dG negative.
Rate of reaction = d[conc]/dt. Order: sum of exponents in rate law. Activation energy: minimum energy for reaction. Catalyst lowers activation energy.
Addition polymers: polyethylene, PVC, polystyrene. Condensation polymers: nylon, polyester, proteins. Natural: rubber, cellulose. Synthetic: plastics, fibers.

entries.title: NEET: Human Genome Project
entries.hook: Stop missing this question in mock tests:
explanations: Completed 2003. Mapped 3 billion base pairs. 20,000-25,000 protein-coding genes. Only 1.5% DNA codes for proteins.
Protecting species in natural habitat. National parks, sanctuaries, biosphere reserves. Example: Jim Corbett National Park.
First: elliptical orbits with Sun at focus. Second: equal areas in equal time. Third: T2 proportional to a3.
Carbonyl group C=O. Aldehydes: terminal carbonyl. Ketones: internal. Nucleophilic addition reactions. Fehling's test distinguishes aldehydes.

entries.title: NEET: Biogeochemical Cycles
entries.hook: High-yield topic for your NEET exam:
explanations: Carbon: photosynthesis + respiration. Nitrogen: fixation, nitrification, assimilation, ammonification, denitrification. Water: evaporation, condensation, precipitation.
Mechanical waves need medium. Transverse: particles perpendicular to wave. Longitudinal: particles parallel. v = f*lambda.
Carbon compounds. Tetravalent. Single bonds: alkane. Double: alkene. Triple:alkyne. Functional groups determine properties. IUPAC naming.
Derivatives of ammonia. Primary: one alkyl group. Secondary: two. Tertiary: three. Quaternary ammonium salts. Basic nature. Diazotization reaction.

entries.title: NEET: Lysosomes
entries.hook: Most students get this wrong in NEET:
explanations: Suicide bags containing hydrolytic enzymes. Digest worn-out organelles and foreign particles. Enzymes work best at acidic pH.
Restriction enzymes cut DNA. Vectors: plasmids, viruses. PCR amplifies DNA. Gel electrophoresis separates. Sequencing reads genetic code.
Oscillating electric and magnetic fields perpendicular to each other and direction of propagation. Speed = c = 3e8 m/s. Light is EM wave.
Valence Shell Electron Pair Repulsion. Electron pairs arrange to minimize repulsion. Determines molecular geometry. Examples: linear, bent, tetrahedral.

entries.title: NEET: Transcription
entries.hook: Stop missing this question in mock tests:
explanations: DNA to mRNA by RNA polymerase. Pre-mRNA capped, tailed, spliced in eukaryotes. In nucleus. Initiation, elongation, termination.
In fluid flow, higher velocity = lower pressure. Explains airplane lift, atomizer, venturi meter. P + 0.5pv2 + pgh = constant.
Heavy nucleus splits into lighter ones releasing energy. Chain reaction. Used in nuclear reactors and atomic bombs. Critical mass needed.
Monosaccharides: glucose, fructose. Disaccharides: sucrose, maltose. Polysaccharides: starch, cellulose. Reducing sugars have free aldehyde group.

entries.title: NEET: CAM Plants
entries.hook: This concept is a must-know for NEET:
explanations: Crassulacean acid metabolism. Stomata open at night to fix CO2 into malate. Day: CO2 released for Calvin cycle. Adaptation for arid conditions.
98.5% O2 bound to hemoglobin as oxyhemoglobin. 1.5% dissolved in plasma. Each hemoglobin carries 4 O2. Dissociation curve is sigmoid.
Rate of reaction = d[conc]/dt. Order: sum of exponents in rate law. Activation energy: minimum energy for reaction. Catalyst lowers activation energy.
-COOH group. Weak acids. Reactions: esterification, reduction, decarboxylation. Acetic acid most common. Formic acid in ant venom.

entries.title: NEET: Immunity Types
entries.hook: Most students get this wrong in NEET:
explanations: Innate: non-specific, from birth. Acquired: specific, after exposure. Active: body produces antibodies. Passive: ready-made antibodies received.
HIV attacks helper T-cells. Transmitted via blood, sex, mother to child. ELISA test detects antibodies. Antiretroviral therapy controls it.
V = IR. Current proportional to voltage at constant temperature. Resistance depends on material, length, cross-section, temperature.
Transition metals (d-block): variable oxidation states, colored compounds, catalytic properties. Lanthanoids and actinoids (f-block): inner transition elements.

entries.title: NEET: Coulomb's Law
entries.hook: This concept is a must-know for NEET:
explanations: F = k(q1q2)/r2. Like charges repel, unlike attract. k = 9e9 Nm2/C2. Electric field E = F/q.
1/f = 1/v - 1/u. Power P = 1/f (diopters). Convex: converging, positive power. Concave: diverging, negative power. Lens maker's formula.
Alcohols: -OH group. Phenols: -OH on benzene. Ethers: R-O-R. Preparation from alkenes, haloalkanes. Oxidation: alcohol to aldehyde to carboxylic acid.
-COOH group. Weak acids. Reactions: esterification, reduction, decarboxylation. Acetic acid most common. Formic acid in ant venom.

entries.title: NEET: Endoplasmic Reticulum
entries.hook: Stop missing this question in mock tests:
explanations: RER has ribosomes and synthesizes proteins. SER lacks ribosomes, synthesizes lipids, detoxifies poisons, stores calcium ions.
Atrial systole 0.1s, ventricular systole 0.3s, joint diastole 0.4s. Total 0.8s at 72 bpm. Heart sounds from valve closure.

entries.title: NEET: Mendel's Laws
entries.hook: This keeps appearing in NEET papers:
explanations: Segregation: alleles separate in gametes. Independent Assortment: genes on different chromosomes assort independently. Dominance: dominant allele masks recessive.
Allele frequencies constant without evolutionary forces. p2 + 2pq + q2 = 1. Affected by mutation, gene flow, drift, selection, non-random mating.
Producers make food. Consumers eat others. Decomposers break down dead matter. Trophic levels form food chains. Energy flow is unidirectional.
Rutherford: nucleus at center with electrons orbiting. Bohr: quantized energy levels. Thomson: plum pudding model. Chadwick: discovered neutron.

entries.title: NEET: Crossing Over
entries.hook: NEET topper secret: master this concept:
explanations: Exchange of genetic material between homologous chromosomes in prophase I of meiosis. Increases genetic variation. Occurs at chiasmata.
In fluid flow, higher velocity = lower pressure. Explains airplane lift, atomizer, venturi meter. P + 0.5pv2 + pgh = constant.
F = qvBsin(theta). Moving charge in magnetic field experiences force. Lorentz force: F = q(E + vxB).
Conductivity between conductor and insulator. Doping: adding impurities. n-type: extra electrons. p-type: holes. PN junction: diode.

entries.title: NEET: Urine Formation
entries.hook: Master this NEET concept once and for all:
explanations: Glomerular filtration 125 mL/min. Tubular reabsorption 99%. Tubular secretion. ADH increases water reabsorption. Aldosterone increases Na+ reabsorption.
Junction rule: sum of currents entering = sum leaving. Loop rule: sum of potential differences in closed loop = 0.

Electrochemical cells convert chemical to electrical energy. Anode: oxidation. Cathode: reduction. EMF = Ered(cathode) - Ered(anode). Nernst equation.

entries.hook: NEET topper secret: master this concept:
explanations: Action potential triggers Ca2+ influx. Neurotransmitters released, bind to receptors on postsynaptic membrane causing excitation or inhibition.
Restriction enzymes cut DNA. Vectors: plasmids, viruses. PCR amplifies DNA. Gel electrophoresis separates. Sequencing reads genetic code.
DC: constant direction. AC: periodically reverses. India: 230V, 50Hz. RMS voltage = peak/sqrt(2). AC can be transformed.

entries.title: NEET: C4 Pathway
entries.hook: Master this NEET concept once and for all:
explanations: Hatch-Slack pathway. CO2 fixed to 4C compound in mesophyll, transferred to bundle sheath. Reduces photorespiration. Example: maize.
Producers make food. Consumers eat others. Decomposers break down dead matter. Trophic levels form food chains. Energy flow is unidirectional.
V = IR. Current proportional to voltage at constant temperature. Resistance depends on material, length, cross-section, temperature.
Gas: no fixed shape/volume. Liquid: fixed volume, no shape. Solid: fixed shape and volume. Ideal gas law: PV = nRT. Real gases deviate at high pressure.

entries.title: NEET: Prokaryotic vs Eukaryotic
entries.hook: High-yield topic for your NEET exam:
explanations: Prokaryotes lack a membrane-bound nucleus. Eukaryotes have a true nucleus and membrane-bound organelles. Bacteria are prokaryotes; plants, animals, fungi are eukaryotes.
Auxins: cell elongation, apical dominance. Gibberellins: stem elongation. Cytokinins: cell division. ABA: stress response. Ethylene: fruit ripening.
Oxidation: loss of electrons. Reduction: gain of electrons. Oxidizing agent accepts electrons. Reducing agent donates electrons. Balancing half-reactions.
Carbonyl group C=O. Aldehydes: terminal carbonyl. Ketones: internal. Nucleophilic addition reactions. Fehling's test distinguishes aldehydes.

entries.title: NEET: Cell Theory
entries.hook: This concept is a must-know for NEET:
explanations: All living organisms are composed of cells. The cell is the basic structural and functional unit of life. All cells arise from pre-existing cells. Proposed by Schleiden, Schwann, and Virchow.
Functional unit of kidney. Bowman's capsule filters blood. PCT reabsorbs nutrients. Loop of Henle concentrates urine. DCT adjusts electrolyte balance.
Hypothalamus controls pituitary. Thyroid: metabolism. Parathyroid: calcium. Adrenal: stress. Pancreas: blood sugar. Gonads: reproduction.
DNA to mRNA by RNA polymerase. Pre-mRNA capped, tailed, spliced in eukaryotes. In nucleus. Initiation, elongation, termination.

entries.script: Fluid Mosaic Model. The plasma membrane is a phospholipid bilayer with embedded proteins. It is fluid and dynamic. Proposed by Singer and Nicolson in 1972. Biogeochemical Cycles. Carbon: photosynthesis + respiration. Nitrogen: fixation, nitrification, assimilation, ammonification, denitrification. Water: evaporation, condensation, precipitation. Work-Energy Theorem. Work = change in kinetic energy. W = 0.5mv2 - 0.5mu2. Work = force x displacement in force direction. Unit: Joule. Aldehydes and Ketones. Carbonyl group C=O. Aldehydes: terminal carbonyl. Ketones: internal. Nucleophilic addition reactions. Fehling's test distinguishes aldehydes.

entries.title: NEET: Transpiration Pull
entries.hook: Most students get this wrong in NEET:
explanations: Water rises in xylem due to transpiration from leaves. Cohesion and adhesion create continuous water column. Main force for water ascent.
P wave: atrial depolarization. QRS: ventricular depolarization. T wave: ventricular repolarization. Records electrical activity of heart.
Insulin lowers blood glucose. Glucagon raises it. Diabetes mellitus from insulin deficiency or resistance.
Junction rule: sum of currents entering = sum leaving. Loop rule: sum of potential differences in closed loop = 0.

entries.title: NEET: Biotechnology Tools
entries.hook: NEET aspirants, listen up:
explanations: Restriction enzymes cut DNA. Vectors: plasmids, viruses. PCR amplifies DNA. Gel electrophoresis separates. Sequencing reads genetic code.
Valence Shell Electron Pair Repulsion. Electron pairs arrange to minimize repulsion. Determines molecular geometry. Examples: linear, bent, tetrahedral.
Mixing of atomic orbitals to form equivalent hybrid orbitals. sp (linear), sp2 (trigonal planar), sp3 (tetrahedral). Explains bond angles.
Polymers of amino acids. Peptide bonds link amino acids. Primary: sequence. Secondary: alpha helix, beta sheet. Tertiary: 3D folding. Quaternary: multiple subunits.

Insulin lowers blood glucose. Glucagon raises it. Diabetes mellitus from insulin deficiency or resistance.
Forward rate = reverse rate. Kc = products/reactants. Le Chatelier's principle: system shifts to oppose change. Effect of temperature, pressure, concentration.
Central metal ion bonded to ligands. Coordination number. Chelate effect. Crystal field theory explains color and magnetism. Important in bioinorganic chemistry.

entries.title: NEET: Kepler's Laws
entries.hook: High-yield topic for your NEET exam:
explanations: First: elliptical orbits with Sun at focus. Second: equal areas in equal time. Third: T2 proportional to a3.
Oscillating electric and magnetic fields perpendicular to each other and direction of propagation. Speed = c = 3e8 m/s. Light is EM wave.
Electrochemical cells convert chemical to electrical energy. Anode: oxidation. Cathode: reduction. EMF = Ered(cathode) - Ered(anode). Nernst equation.
Alkanes: CnH2n+2, saturated. Alkenes: CnH2n, unsaturated. Alkynes: CnH2n-2. Aromatic: benzene ring. Combustion, substitution, addition reactions.

entries.title: NEET: Prokaryotic vs Eukaryotic
entries.hook: Most students get this wrong in NEET:
explanations: Prokaryotes lack a membrane-bound nucleus. Eukaryotes have a true nucleus and membrane-bound organelles. Bacteria are prokaryotes; plants, animals, fungi are eukaryotes.
Ionic: electron transfer, electrostatic attraction. Covalent: electron sharing. Metallic: delocalized electrons. VSEPR theory predicts shape.
-COOH group. Weak acids. Reactions: esterification, reduction, decarboxylation. Acetic acid most common. Formic acid in ant venom.
Transition metals (d-block): variable oxidation states, colored compounds, catalytic properties. Lanthanoids and actinoids (f-block): inner transition elements.

entries.title: NEET: Photosynthesis Light Reaction
entries.hook: High-yield topic for your NEET exam:
explanations: Occurs in thylakoids. Light splits water producing O2, ATP, NADPH. Photosystems I and II work in Z-scheme.
Auxins: cell elongation, apical dominance. Gibberellins: stem elongation. Cytokinins: cell division. ABA: stress response. Ethylene: fruit ripening.
Inspiration: diaphragm contracts, intercostal muscles lift ribs. Expiration: passive relaxation. Intrapleural pressure changes drive air flow.
V = IR. Current proportional to voltage at constant temperature. Resistance depends on material, length, cross-section, temperature.

entries.title: NEET: Cell Cycle
entries.hook: Stop missing this question in mock tests:
explanations: Interphase (G1, S, G2) prepares for division. Mitosis divides nucleus. Cytokinesis divides cytoplasm. G0 is resting phase.
98.5% O2 bound to hemoglobin as oxyhemoglobin. 1.5% dissolved in plasma. Each hemoglobin carries 4 O2. Dissociation curve is sigmoid.
Functional unit of kidney. Bowman's capsule filters blood. PCT reabsorbs nutrients. Loop of Henle concentrates urine. DCT adjusts electrolyte balance.
Rate of reaction = d[conc]/dt. Order: sum of exponents in rate law. Activation energy: minimum energy for reaction. Catalyst lowers activation energy.

entries.hook: High-yield topic for your NEET exam:

Dark reaction in stroma. CO2 fixed to glucose using ATP and NADPH. Stages: carboxylation, reduction, regeneration of RuBP.
P wave: atrial depolarization. QRS: ventricular depolarization. T wave: ventricular repolarization. Records electrical activity of heart.

entries.title: NEET: Nucleus
entries.hook: Most students get this wrong in NEET:
explanations: Contains genetic material (DNA) surrounded by double membrane nuclear envelope with pores. Nucleolus is site of ribosome synthesis.
Interphase (G1, S, G2) prepares for division. Mitosis divides nucleus. Cytokinesis divides cytoplasm. G0 is resting phase.
Segregation: alleles separate in gametes. Independent Assortment: genes on different chromosomes assort independently. Dominance: dominant allele masks recessive.
Carbonyl group C=O. Aldehydes: terminal carbonyl. Ketones: internal. Nucleophilic addition reactions. Fehling's test distinguishes aldehydes.

entries.title: NEET: Plant Growth Regulators
entries.hook: This concept is a must-know for NEET:
explanations: Auxins: cell elongation, apical dominance. Gibberellins: stem elongation. Cytokinins: cell division. ABA: stress response. Ethylene: fruit ripening.
Semiconservative. Helicase unwinds. Polymerase adds nucleotides. Leading: continuous. Lagging: Okazaki fragments. Ligase joins fragments.
Inducible operon. Lactose absent: repressor blocks transcription. Lactose present: binds repressor, allows lac gene transcription.
F = k(q1q2)/r2. Like charges repel, unlike attract. k = 9e9 Nm2/C2. Electric field E = F/q.

Energy levels: En = -13.6/n2 eV. Transitions produce spectral lines. Semi-conductors. Conductivity between conductor and insulator. Doping: adding impurities. n-type: extra electrons. p-type: holes. PN junction: diode.

entries.title: NEET: Fluid Mosaic Model
entries.hook: Master this NEET concept once and for all:
explanations: The plasma membrane is a phospholipid bilayer with embedded proteins. It is fluid and dynamic. Proposed by Singer and Nicolson in 1972.
Protecting species in natural habitat. National parks, sanctuaries, biosphere reserves. Example: Jim Corbett National Park.
Mechanical waves need medium. Transverse: particles perpendicular to wave. Longitudinal: particles parallel. v = f*lambda.
F = k(q1q2)/r2. Like charges repel, unlike attract. k = 9e9 Nm2/C2. Electric field E = F/q.

entries.title: NEET: Lysosomes
entries.hook: High-yield topic for your NEET exam:
explanations: Suicide bags containing hydrolytic enzymes. Digest worn-out organelles and foreign particles. Enzymes work best at acidic pH.
Exchange of genetic material between homologous chromosomes in prophase I of meiosis. Increases genetic variation. Occurs at chiasmata.
DNA to mRNA by RNA polymerase. Pre-mRNA capped, tailed, spliced in eukaryotes. In nucleus. Initiation, elongation, termination.
Light ejects electrons from metal surface. Einstein: light as photons, E = hf. Kinetic energy of ejected electron = hf - work function.

entries.hook: High-yield topic for your NEET exam:
explanations: Double-membrane organelle with inner membrane folded into cristae. Powerhouse of the cell producing ATP via oxidative phosphorylation. Contains its own DNA.
Australopithecus 4 mya, Homo habilis 2.4 mya, Homo erectus 1.8 mya, Neanderthals 0.4 mya, Homo sapiens 0.3 mya. Originated in Africa.
Protecting species in natural habitat. National parks, sanctuaries, biosphere reserves. Example: Jim Corbett National Park.
Alcohols: -OH group. Phenols: -OH on benzene. Ethers: R-O-R. Preparation from alkenes, haloalkanes. Oxidation: alcohol to aldehyde to carboxylic acid.

entries.title: NEET: Ohm's Law
entries.hook: Stop missing this question in mock tests:
explanations: V = IR. Current proportional to voltage at constant temperature. Resistance depends on material, length, cross-section, temperature.
Electrochemical cells convert chemical to electrical energy. Anode: oxidation. Cathode: reduction. EMF = Ered(cathode) - Ered(anode). Nernst equation.
Alkanes: CnH2n+2, saturated. Alkenes: CnH2n, unsaturated. Alkynes: CnH2n-2. Aromatic: benzene ring. Combustion, substitution, addition reactions.
Transition metals (d-block): variable oxidation states, colored compounds, catalytic properties. Lanthanoids and actinoids (f-block): inner transition elements.

entries.title: NEET: Human Evolution
entries.hook: Most students get this wrong in NEET:
explanations: Australopithecus 4 mya, Homo habilis 2.4 mya, Homo erectus 1.8 mya, Neanderthals 0.4 mya, Homo sapiens 0.3 mya. Originated in Africa.
HIV attacks helper T-cells. Transmitted via blood, sex, mother to child. ELISA test detects antibodies. Antiretroviral therapy controls it.
Uncontrolled cell division from genetic mutations. Proto-oncogenes promote growth. Tumor suppressors inhibit. Treatments: surgery, radiation, chemo, immunotherapy.
Heavy nucleus splits into lighter ones releasing energy. Chain reaction. Used in nuclear reactors and atomic bombs. Critical mass needed.

entries.title: NEET: Prokaryotic vs Eukaryotic
entries.hook: Stop missing this question in mock tests:
explanations: Prokaryotes lack a membrane-bound nucleus. Eukaryotes have a true nucleus and membrane-bound organelles. Bacteria are prokaryotes; plants, animals, fungi are eukaryotes.
Dark reaction in stroma. CO2 fixed to glucose using ATP and NADPH. Stages: carboxylation, reduction, regeneration of RuBP.
Enthalpy H = U + PV. Exothermic: dH negative. Endothermic: dH positive. Gibbs free energy dG = dH - TdS. Spontaneous when dG negative.
Addition polymers: polyethylene, PVC, polystyrene. Condensation polymers: nylon, polyester, proteins. Natural: rubber, cellulose. Synthetic: plastics, fibers.

entries.title: NEET: Endoplasmic Reticulum
entries.hook: This keeps appearing in NEET papers:
explanations: RER has ribosomes and synthesizes proteins. SER lacks ribosomes, synthesizes lipids, detoxifies poisons, stores calcium ions.
Dendrites receive signals. Cell body integrates. Axon transmits. Myelin sheath speeds conduction. Synapse is junction between neurons.
Electrons in quantized orbits. Angular momentum = nh/2pi. Energy levels: En = -13.6/n2 eV. Transitions produce spectral lines.
-COOH group. Weak acids. Reactions: esterification, reduction, decarboxylation. Acetic acid most common. Formic acid in ant venom.

entries.title: NEET: Nephron
entries.hook: This concept is a must-know for NEET:
explanations: Functional unit of kidney. Bowman's capsule filters blood. PCT reabsorbs nutrients. Loop of Henle concentrates urine. DCT adjusts electrolyte balance.
Segregation: alleles separate in gametes. Independent Assortment: genes on different chromosomes assort independently. Dominance: dominant allele masks recessive.
Restriction enzymes cut DNA. Vectors: plasmids, viruses. PCR amplifies DNA. Gel electrophoresis separates. Sequencing reads genetic code.
Groups 13-18. Boron family to noble gases. Variable oxidation states. Trends in metallic character, ionization energy, electronegativity down groups.

entries.title: NEET: Hardy-Weinberg Principle
entries.hook: NEET aspirants, listen up:
explanations: Allele frequencies constant without evolutionary forces. p2 + 2pq + q2 = 1. Affected by mutation, gene flow, drift, selection, non-random mating.
Producers make food. Consumers eat others. Decomposers break down dead matter. Trophic levels form food chains. Energy flow is unidirectional.
Energy cannot be created or destroyed, only converted. Total mechanical energy (KE+PE) constant without non-conservative forces.
Transition metals (d-block): variable oxidation states, colored compounds, catalytic properties. Lanthanoids and actinoids (f-block): inner transition elements.

entries.title: NEET: Digestive System
entries.hook: This keeps appearing in NEET papers:
explanations: Alimentary canal: mouth to anus. Accessory organs: liver, pancreas, gallbladder. Mechanical and chemical breakdown of food.
Hypothalamus controls pituitary. Thyroid: metabolism. Parathyroid: calcium. Adrenal: stress. Pancreas: blood sugar. Gonads: reproduction.
Carbon compounds. Tetravalent. Single bonds: alkane. Double: alkene. Triple:alkyne. Functional groups determine properties. IUPAC naming.
Monosaccharides: glucose, fructose. Disaccharides: sucrose, maltose. Polysaccharides: starch, cellulose. Reducing sugars have free aldehyde group.

entries.title: NEET: Transpiration Pull
entries.hook: Master this NEET concept once and for all:
explanations: Water rises in xylem due to transpiration from leaves. Cohesion and adhesion create continuous water column. Main force for water ascent.
Dendrites receive signals. Cell body integrates. Axon transmits. Myelin sheath speeds conduction. Synapse is junction between neurons.
Regions with high endemic species under threat. India has 4: Western Ghats, Eastern Himalayas, Indo-Burma, Sundaland.
DC: constant direction. AC: periodically reverses. India: 230V, 50Hz. RMS voltage = peak/sqrt(2). AC can be transformed.

entries.title: NEET: Faraday's Law
entries.hook: High-yield topic for your NEET exam:
explanations: Changing magnetic flux induces EMF. EMF = -d(flux)/dt. Lenz's law: induced current opposes change. Basis of generators.
Ionic: electron transfer, electrostatic attraction. Covalent: electron sharing. Metallic: delocalized electrons. VSEPR theory predicts shape.

entries.title: NEET: Lac Operon
entries.hook: Stop missing this question in mock tests:
explanations: Inducible operon. Lactose absent: repressor blocks transcription. Lactose present: binds repressor, allows lac gene transcription.
Restriction enzymes cut DNA. Vectors: plasmids, viruses. PCR amplifies DNA. Gel electrophoresis separates. Sequencing reads genetic code.
Carbonyl group C=O. Aldehydes: terminal carbonyl. Ketones: internal. Nucleophilic addition reactions. Fehling's test distinguishes aldehydes.
Derivatives of ammonia. Primary: one alkyl group. Secondary: two. Tertiary: three. Quaternary ammonium salts. Basic nature. Diazotization reaction.

entries.title: NEET: Cell Theory

explanations: All living organisms are composed of cells. The cell is the basic structural and functional unit of life. All cells arise from pre-existing cells. Proposed by Schleiden, Schwann, and Virchow.
DC: constant direction. AC: periodically reverses. India: 230V, 50Hz. RMS voltage = peak/sqrt(2). AC can be transformed.
1/f = 1/v - 1/u. Power P = 1/f (diopters). Convex: converging, positive power. Concave: diverging, negative power. Lens maker's formula.
Carbon compounds. Tetravalent. Single bonds: alkane. Double: alkene. Triple:alkyne. Functional groups determine properties. IUPAC naming.

explanations: Hatch-Slack pathway. CO2 fixed to 4C compound in mesophyll, transferred to bundle sheath. Reduces photorespiration. Example: maize.
Inspiration: diaphragm contracts, intercostal muscles lift ribs. Expiration: passive relaxation. Intrapleural pressure changes drive air flow.

entries.title: NEET: Immunity Types
entries.hook: Stop missing this question in mock tests:
explanations: Innate: non-specific, from birth. Acquired: specific, after exposure. Active: body produces antibodies. Passive: ready-made antibodies received.
Carbonyl group C=O. Aldehydes: terminal carbonyl. Ketones: internal. Nucleophilic addition reactions. Fehling's test distinguishes aldehydes.
-COOH group. Weak acids. Reactions: esterification, reduction, decarboxylation. Acetic acid most common. Formic acid in ant venom.
Monosaccharides: glucose, fructose. Disaccharides: sucrose, maltose. Polysaccharides: starch, cellulose. Reducing sugars have free aldehyde group.

entries.script: Cell Cycle. Interphase (G1, S, G2) prepares for division. Mitosis divides nucleus. Cytokinesis divides cytoplasm. G0 is resting phase. DNA Structure. Double helix. Nucleotides: phosphate, deoxyribose, base (A,T,G,C). A=T (2 bonds), G≡C (3 bonds). Antiparallel strands. Recombinant DNA. Gene inserted into vector. Vector introduced to host. Host expresses desired protein. Used for insulin, growth hormone, vaccines. Biomolecules: Carbohydrates. Monosaccharides: glucose, fructose. Disaccharides: sucrose, maltose. Polysaccharides: starch, cellulose. Reducing sugars have free aldehyde group.

entries.title: NEET: C4 Pathway
entries.hook: NEET aspirants, listen up:
explanations: Hatch-Slack pathway. CO2 fixed to 4C compound in mesophyll, transferred to bundle sheath. Reduces photorespiration. Example: maize.
Water rises in xylem due to transpiration from leaves. Cohesion and adhesion create continuous water column. Main force for water ascent.
Inspiration: diaphragm contracts, intercostal muscles lift ribs. Expiration: passive relaxation. Intrapleural pressure changes drive air flow.
Central metal ion bonded to ligands. Coordination number. Chelate effect. Crystal field theory explains color and magnetism. Important in bioinorganic chemistry.

entries.title: NEET: Lac Operon
entries.hook: NEET topper secret: master this concept:
explanations: Inducible operon. Lactose absent: repressor blocks transcription. Lactose present: binds repressor, allows lac gene transcription.
V = IR. Current proportional to voltage at constant temperature. Resistance depends on material, length, cross-section, temperature.
Rutherford: nucleus at center with electrons orbiting. Bohr: quantized energy levels. Thomson: plum pudding model. Chadwick: discovered neutron.
Raoult's law: vapor pressure lowering. Boiling point elevation. Freezing point depression. Osmotic pressure. dTb = Kb*m. i = van't Hoff factor.

entries.title: NEET: Oxygen Transport
entries.hook: Master this NEET concept once and for all:
explanations: 98.5% O2 bound to hemoglobin as oxyhemoglobin. 1.5% dissolved in plasma. Each hemoglobin carries 4 O2. Dissociation curve is sigmoid.
F = qvBsin(theta). Moving charge in magnetic field experiences force. Lorentz force: F = q(E + vxB).
Addition polymers: polyethylene, PVC, polystyrene. Condensation polymers: nylon, polyester, proteins. Natural: rubber, cellulose. Synthetic: plastics, fibers.
Gas: no fixed shape/volume. Liquid: fixed volume, no shape. Solid: fixed shape and volume. Ideal gas law: PV = nRT. Real gases deviate at high pressure.

entries.title: NEET: Photorespiration
entries.hook: NEET aspirants, listen up:
explanations: RuBisCO fixes O2 instead of CO2 producing toxic compound. Wasteful process reducing photosynthesis. Common in C3 plants under hot dry conditions.
Atrial systole 0.1s, ventricular systole 0.3s, joint diastole 0.4s. Total 0.8s at 72 bpm. Heart sounds from valve closure.
Completed 2003. Mapped 3 billion base pairs. 20,000-25,000 protein-coding genes. Only 1.5% DNA codes for proteins.
Entropy of universe always increases. Heat cannot spontaneously flow from cold to hot. Perpetual motion machines impossible.

Carbonyl group C=O. Aldehydes: terminal carbonyl. Ketones: internal. Nucleophilic addition reactions. Fehling's test distinguishes aldehydes.

entries.title: NEET: Mitochondria
entries.hook: This concept is a must-know for NEET:
explanations: Double-membrane organelle with inner membrane folded into cristae. Powerhouse of the cell producing ATP via oxidative phosphorylation. Contains its own DNA.
Segregation: alleles separate in gametes. Independent Assortment: genes on different chromosomes assort independently. Dominance: dominant allele masks recessive.
In fluid flow, higher velocity = lower pressure. Explains airplane lift, atomizer, venturi meter. P + 0.5pv2 + pgh = constant.
Most efficient heat engine. Efficiency = 1 - T2/T1. Reversible cyclic process. Carnot cycle: isothermal + adiabatic expansions and compressions.

entries.title: NEET: Biogeochemical Cycles
entries.hook: This concept is a must-know for NEET:
explanations: Carbon: photosynthesis + respiration. Nitrogen: fixation, nitrification, assimilation, ammonification, denitrification. Water: evaporation, condensation, precipitation.
Angle of incidence = angle of reflection. Mirror formula: 1/f = 1/u + 1/v. Concave: real/inverted. Convex: virtual/upright.
Polymers of amino acids. Peptide bonds link amino acids. Primary: sequence. Secondary: alpha helix, beta sheet. Tertiary: 3D folding. Quaternary: multiple subunits.
Transition metals (d-block): variable oxidation states, colored compounds, catalytic properties. Lanthanoids and actinoids (f-block): inner transition elements.

entries.title: NEET: Cell Theory
entries.hook: High-yield topic for your NEET exam:
explanations: All living organisms are composed of cells. The cell is the basic structural and functional unit of life. All cells arise from pre-existing cells. Proposed by Schleiden, Schwann, and Virchow.
Interphase (G1, S, G2) prepares for division. Mitosis divides nucleus. Cytokinesis divides cytoplasm. G0 is resting phase.
Exchange of genetic material between homologous chromosomes in prophase I of meiosis. Increases genetic variation. Occurs at chiasmata.
Inspiration: diaphragm contracts, intercostal muscles lift ribs. Expiration: passive relaxation. Intrapleural pressure changes drive air flow.

entries.title: NEET: Lysosomes
entries.hook: Master this NEET concept once and for all:
explanations: Suicide bags containing hydrolytic enzymes. Digest worn-out organelles and foreign particles. Enzymes work best at acidic pH.
Auxins: cell elongation, apical dominance. Gibberellins: stem elongation. Cytokinins: cell division. ABA: stress response. Ethylene: fruit ripening.
Functional unit of kidney. Bowman's capsule filters blood. PCT reabsorbs nutrients. Loop of Henle concentrates urine. DCT adjusts electrolyte balance.
Dendrites receive signals. Cell body integrates. Axon transmits. Myelin sheath speeds conduction. Synapse is junction between neurons.

entries.title: NEET: Crossing Over
entries.hook: This concept is a must-know for NEET:
explanations: Exchange of genetic material between homologous chromosomes in prophase I of meiosis. Increases genetic variation. Occurs at chiasmata.
Restriction enzymes cut DNA. Vectors: plasmids, viruses. PCR amplifies DNA. Gel electrophoresis separates. Sequencing reads genetic code.
Oscillating electric and magnetic fields perpendicular to each other and direction of propagation. Speed = c = 3e8 m/s. Light is EM wave.
Forward rate = reverse rate. Kc = products/reactants. Le Chatelier's principle: system shifts to oppose change. Effect of temperature, pressure, concentration.

entries.title: NEET: Mitochondria
entries.hook: High-yield topic for your NEET exam:
explanations: Double-membrane organelle with inner membrane folded into cristae. Powerhouse of the cell producing ATP via oxidative phosphorylation. Contains its own DNA.
Contains genetic material (DNA) surrounded by double membrane nuclear envelope with pores. Nucleolus is site of ribosome synthesis.
Functional unit of kidney. Bowman's capsule filters blood. PCT reabsorbs nutrients. Loop of Henle concentrates urine. DCT adjusts electrolyte balance.
Polymers of amino acids. Peptide bonds link amino acids. Primary: sequence. Secondary: alpha helix, beta sheet. Tertiary: 3D folding. Quaternary: multiple subunits.

entries.title: NEET: Mitosis vs Meiosis
entries.hook: This keeps appearing in NEET papers:
explanations: Mitosis: 2 identical diploid cells for growth. Meiosis: 4 non-identical haploid gametes with two divisions. Meiosis creates genetic variation.
Hypothalamus controls pituitary. Thyroid: metabolism. Parathyroid: calcium. Adrenal: stress. Pancreas: blood sugar. Gonads: reproduction.
Producers make food. Consumers eat others. Decomposers break down dead matter. Trophic levels form food chains. Energy flow is unidirectional.
First: elliptical orbits with Sun at focus. Second: equal areas in equal time. Third: T2 proportional to a3.

entries.title: NEET: Mendel's Laws
entries.hook: Stop missing this question in mock tests:
explanations: Segregation: alleles separate in gametes. Independent Assortment: genes on different chromosomes assort independently. Dominance: dominant allele masks recessive.
Allele frequencies constant without evolutionary forces. p2 + 2pq + q2 = 1. Affected by mutation, gene flow, drift, selection, non-random mating.
Energy cannot be created or destroyed, only converted. Total mechanical energy (KE+PE) constant without non-conservative forces.
In fluid flow, higher velocity = lower pressure. Explains airplane lift, atomizer, venturi meter. P + 0.5pv2 + pgh = constant.

explanations: Modifies, sorts, and packages proteins. Receives from ER at cis face, releases from trans face. Discovered by Camillo Golgi.
Insulin lowers blood glucose. Glucagon raises it. Diabetes mellitus from insulin deficiency or resistance.
Producers make food. Consumers eat others. Decomposers break down dead matter. Trophic levels form food chains. Energy flow is unidirectional.
Junction rule: sum of currents entering = sum leaving. Loop rule: sum of potential differences in closed loop = 0.

entries.title: NEET: CAM Plants
entries.hook: Master this NEET concept once and for all:
explanations: Crassulacean acid metabolism. Stomata open at night to fix CO2 into malate. Day: CO2 released for Calvin cycle. Adaptation for arid conditions.
Glomerular filtration 125 mL/min. Tubular reabsorption 99%. Tubular secretion. ADH increases water reabsorption. Aldosterone increases Na+ reabsorption.
Completed 2003. Mapped 3 billion base pairs. 20,000-25,000 protein-coding genes. Only 1.5% DNA codes for proteins.

explanations: Modifies, sorts, and packages proteins. Receives from ER at cis face, releases from trans face. Discovered by Camillo Golgi.
Dark reaction in stroma. CO2 fixed to glucose using ATP and NADPH. Stages: carboxylation, reduction, regeneration of RuBP.
Double helix. Nucleotides: phosphate, deoxyribose, base (A,T,G,C). A=T (2 bonds), G≡C (3 bonds). Antiparallel strands.
Rate of reaction = d[conc]/dt. Order: sum of exponents in rate law. Activation energy: minimum energy for reaction. Catalyst lowers activation energy.
